[2026-05-20 17:07] Hopper, Brunel here. Welcome — first dispatch. Aen has sanctioned this package; standing by to support.

This message is the **primary artifact** per `relay-to-primary-artifact-fidelity-discipline.md`. Your spawn prompt carried a Stage-1 relay-fold of this same text. Cross-walk: any divergence between the relay-fold in your spawn context and this primary artifact resolves to the primary. Quote THIS message verbatim in your operations-log entry's sanction-status field.

This is the apex-research authorized_keys multi-key fix, PHASE 1 PREPARATION ONLY. NO CONTAINER RECREATE. Phase 2 (recreate + verification) is a separate future dispatch contingent on apex team's maintenance-window approval.

Substrate target: apex-research (FR-shipped). Operations log: docs/operations-log-2026-05.md (create file if not present).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND (for your read; not a step)

PO reports: in the live apex-research container, /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys contains TWO keys (PO's + Aleksandr's). On container rebuild, only PO's key survives. We want Aleksandr's to also persist across rebuilds.

Diagnosis (FR-side, confirmed via deployed-artifacts read on Brunel side):
- Entrypoint Step 7 (entrypoint-apex.sh:169-189) regenerates authorized_keys on every container start from SSH_PUBLIC_KEY* env vars.
- Multi-key support is already in place: docker-compose.yml:53-54 passes both SSH_PUBLIC_KEY and SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 from host-side .env.
- Gap: host-side .env on the RC bare-metal host only sets SSH_PUBLIC_KEY. Aleksandr's key lives only on the ephemeral container layer (added via docker exec or similar), which is wiped on rebuild.

Phase 1 stages the fix: extract Aleksandr's current pubkey, append SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 line to .env, verify .env parses. Phase 2 will recreate the container under a sanctioned maintenance window.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
READ THESE DEPLOYED-ARTIFACTS BEFORE EXECUTING (your discipline; required audit declaration)

- designs/deployed/apex-research/container/entrypoint-apex.sh — Step 7 lines 169-189 (env-var enumeration into authorized_keys)
- designs/deployed/apex-research/container/docker-compose.yml — lines 47-60 (env block, both SSH_PUBLIC_KEY and _2 wired)
- designs/deployed/apex-research/container/.env.example — line 13 (template shows only the SSH_PUBLIC_KEY slot)

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUBSTRATE-ACCESS SHAPES YOU WILL USE

- Container-user SSH (for P1.1 only): `ssh -i $env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_apex -p 2222 ai-teams@100.96.54.170 '<cmd>'`
- Host-user SSH (for all other steps): `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 '<cmd>'`
  - `-T` flag: explicitly disables TTY allocation. Required for non-interactive command execution to keep stdout clean and prevent PowerShell line-ending hazards.
  - Default key from `~/.ssh/id_ed25519` (not apex-specific).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STEPS (sequential, hard-gate between each — do not proceed if a step's pass criterion is not met)

P1.1 [Tier R, container-user SSH port 2222] EXTRACT Aleksandr's pubkey from live container.

  Exact PowerShell pipeline:

    $allKeys = ssh -i $env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_apex -p 2222 ai-teams@100.96.54.170 'cat /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys'
    $nonPoLines = @($allKeys | Where-Object { $_ -notmatch 'michelek\s*$' -and $_.Trim() -ne '' })
    if ($nonPoLines.Count -ne 1) {
        Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] Expected exactly 1 non-PO key, found $($nonPoLines.Count). Aborting dispatch."
        # halt — do not proceed to P1.2
    }
    $alekKey = $nonPoLines[0].TrimEnd("`r","`n"," ","`t")

  Pass criterion: $alekKey is set to a non-empty single-line string; $nonPoLines.Count -eq 1.
  Failure modes & surface-back:
    - 0 non-PO lines → diagnosis is wrong (PO said key is in container right now); surface to Brunel.
    - 2+ non-PO lines → unexpected state; capture all non-PO lines, surface to Brunel for triage.
  The @() force-array idiom is load-bearing — without it, a single match returns a string and $nonPoLines.Count returns the string length.
  The TrimEnd handles the line-ending issue at the source; everything downstream assumes $alekKey is clean.

P1.2 [Tier R, host-user SSH] LOCATE apex compose dir + confirm .env writability on the RC bare-metal host.

  Locate-probe:
    ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 'ls -la ~/apex-research/ 2>/dev/null || ls -la ~/docker/apex-research/ 2>/dev/null || ls -la ~/ai-teams/apex-research/ 2>/dev/null; echo "---FIND---"; find ~/ -maxdepth 4 -name docker-compose.yml -path "*apex*" 2>/dev/null'

  Pass criterion: exactly one directory contains docker-compose.yml + .env for apex-research. Capture as $COMPOSE_DIR (absolute path).
  Failure modes & surface-back:
    - 0 matches → surface to Brunel; PO may need to clarify operational compose-dir.
    - 2+ matches → surface to Brunel with all matches; do not guess.

  .env writability probe (always run):
    ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "ls -la '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'"

  Pass criterion: file exists and is owned/writable by `dev`. Surface back if not.

  NOTE: PO directed this be a probe (not pre-baked) precisely so you capture the path in your scratchpad — see P1.6.5 below. PO's framing: "let Hopper probe and save it as personal skill."

P1.3 [Tier M — Brunel single-line ack] BACKUP .env to timestamped copy.

  Collision check + dynamic suffix:

    $bakBase = ".env.bak.2026-05-20"
    $exists = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "test -f '$COMPOSE_DIR/$bakBase' && echo EXISTS || echo OK"
    if ($exists.Trim() -eq "EXISTS") {
        $now = Get-Date -Format "HHmm"
        $bakName = "$bakBase-$now"
    } else {
        $bakName = $bakBase
    }
    ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '$COMPOSE_DIR' && cp .env '$bakName' && ls -la .env '$bakName'"

  Pass criterion: ls output shows both .env and the backup file, identical sizes, backup timestamp newer.
  Note: capture the chosen $bakName for inclusion in the operations-log entry — Phase 2 rollback references this filename.

  Tasker ack (Brunel): "I sanction this Tier M op. The compose system ignores non-.env-named files in the same dir; the backup file is invisible to docker compose. Operation appends one file, does not modify any existing file, fully reversible by deletion. — Brunel"

P1.4 [Tier M — Brunel single-line ack] APPEND SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 line to .env via base64-transit pattern.

  Why base64 transit: PowerShell-on-Windows stdout-pipes can inject CRLF depending on $OutputEncoding + PSVersion. A stray \r in $SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 would land in authorized_keys at next rebuild and sshd silently rejects malformed lines — Aleksandr's key would fail to install with no diagnostic surface until Phase 2 verification. Base64 transit eliminates this class entirely: the pubkey crosses the wire as a single base64 token, reconstituted server-side via `base64 -d`, never word-split or shell-expanded.

  Exact PowerShell:

    $alekKeyB64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($alekKey))
    $remoteCmd = "KEY=`$(echo '$alekKeyB64' | base64 -d); printf 'SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=\`"%s\`"\n' `"`$KEY`" >> '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'"
    ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 $remoteCmd

  Reading the remote command after PowerShell interpolation expands $alekKeyB64 and $COMPOSE_DIR:

    KEY=$(echo '<base64-token>' | base64 -d); printf 'SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="%s"\n' "$KEY" >> '/path/to/.env'

  Remote-side: bash decodes the base64 into KEY (no word-splitting because base64 alphabet has no shell-special chars); printf emits the well-formed env line with an explicit LF; >> appends. Single ssh round-trip, no scp/tempfile ceremony.

  Pass criterion: ssh exit code 0, no stderr output.
  Failure modes & surface-back:
    - Non-zero exit → surface to Brunel with the exact stderr.
    - PowerShell quoting interpolation produces unexpected $remoteCmd → print $remoteCmd before invoking ssh for sanity check; surface if it doesn't look like the example above.

  Tasker ack (Brunel): "I sanction this Tier M op. Appends one well-formed env line; does not modify any existing line; fully reversible by restoring from P1.3 backup. Base64-transit eliminates PowerShell line-ending corruption. — Brunel"

  FALLBACK PATTERN (if base64-transit fails for any reason):
    Write the literal line to a local tempfile with explicit LF line-ending, scp to /tmp on RC, ssh-append-then-rm. Documented here for record; do not switch to it without surfacing back to Brunel first.

P1.5 [Tier R, host-user SSH] VERIFY .env state — both keys present + byte-equality of _2 value.

  Read back:
    $envLine = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "grep '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=' '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'"
    $envValue = $envLine -replace '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="(.+)"$', '$1'

  Byte-equality check:
    if ($envValue -ne $alekKey) {
        Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] .env _2 value mismatches P1.1 capture. Phase 2 would silently fail. Restoring from backup and aborting."
        ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '$COMPOSE_DIR' && cp '$bakName' .env"
        # surface to Brunel with: hex-dump of $alekKey, hex-dump of $envValue, the diff
    }

  Also verify both keys present at all:
    $bothKeys = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "grep -c '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY' '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'"
    if ([int]$bothKeys -ne 2) {
        Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] Expected 2 SSH_PUBLIC_KEY lines in .env, found $bothKeys. Restoring."
        # restore + surface
    }

  Pass criterion: both checks pass — exactly 2 SSH_PUBLIC_KEY* lines AND _2 value byte-equal to $alekKey.
  This step catches both "transit corrupted the value" and "Hopper appended to wrong file" silent failure modes before they propagate to Phase 2.

P1.5b [Tier R, host-user SSH] CONFIRM .env parses cleanly under docker compose — without touching the running container.

  Command:
    ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '$COMPOSE_DIR' && docker compose config 2>&1 | grep SSH_PUBLIC_KEY"

  Pass criterion: rendered compose output includes both `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY=...` and `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=...` with their resolved values; zero stderr; ssh exit code 0. `docker compose config` is read-only — does NOT start, stop, restart, or recreate the container.
  Failure mode & rollback:
    - If docker compose config errors out (malformed .env from P1.4 quoting accident): the running container is unaffected (it's already running on its previous env). Rollback `.env`:
        ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '$COMPOSE_DIR' && cp '$bakName' .env"
      Verify rollback with grep, then surface to Brunel.

P1.6 [Tier R + log + report] CLOSE Phase 1 — write operations log + report to taskers.

  Operations-log entry (docs/operations-log-2026-05.md, append-only — create file if first May 2026 dispatch):

    Required fields per your discipline:
    - timestamp (ISO 8601)
    - tasker: Brunel
    - dispatch summary: "apex-research authorized_keys Phase 1 prep — stage SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 in host-side .env for future rebuild to pick up. NO RECREATE this dispatch."
    - tier classification + sanction status: P1.1=R; P1.2=R; P1.3=M (Brunel ack quoted verbatim from this dispatch); P1.4=M (Brunel ack quoted verbatim from this dispatch); P1.5=R; P1.5b=R.
    - deployed-artifacts-read declaration: paths and line ranges per the READ section above.
    - commands executed: verbatim ssh invocations + remote commands for each P1.X step.
    - outputs: relevant excerpts — P1.2 found $COMPOSE_DIR (cite path), P1.3 produced $bakName (cite), P1.5b output of docker compose config.
    - outcome: "Phase 1 staged; .env updated with SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2; Phase 2 (recreate) awaits maintenance-window sanction."

  Report to Brunel (close diagnosis loop) and CC Aen (team-level role-of-record):
    - Subject: Phase 1 outcome + ops-log path.
    - Include: $COMPOSE_DIR, $bakName, any non-pass-criterion observations.
    - Status line: "Ready for Phase 2 dispatch when apex team approves maintenance window."

P1.6.5 [Scratchpad — substrate-knowledge accumulation] RECORD durable substrate facts learned this dispatch.

  This is the Hopper-side discipline PO articulated as "personal skill" — first-ever dispatch against a substrate teaches you durable operational facts (paths, port numbers, named-volume layouts, file-ownership defaults) that will not change between dispatches. Capture them in your scratchpad so future-Hopper consults the scratchpad before re-probing.

  For this dispatch, write to teams/framework-research/memory/hopper.md under a `[LEARNED — substrate, apex-research]` block:

    [LEARNED — substrate, apex-research] Compose dir on RC bare-metal host: <captured $COMPOSE_DIR value>. Discovered via P1.2 locate-probe on first-ever dispatch (Phase 1 authorized_keys prep, 2026-05-20). Future dispatches against apex-research host-side can bake this path directly instead of re-probing.

    [LEARNED — substrate, apex-research] Host-user SSH shape: `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170` (default key, port 22, no ProxyJump). Source-of-truth: ~/bin/rc-deployments.json entry num:"1". Use raw ssh form (not rc-connect wrapper) for non-interactive ops.

    [LEARNED — substrate, apex-research] Container-user SSH shape: `ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_apex -p 2222 ai-teams@100.96.54.170`. Source-of-truth: ~/bin/rc-deployments.json entry num:"2".

    [LEARNED — substrate, apex-research] .env owner on host: <captured from P1.2 ls -la output, e.g. dev:dev>. Backup-file naming convention used: `.env.bak.YYYY-MM-DD` with -HHMM suffix on same-day collision.

  Generalized discipline (apply on any first-ever dispatch against a new substrate):

  On the first dispatch you receive against any FR-shipped substrate, scratchpad-capture as `[LEARNED — substrate, <team-name>]` entries:

  - Host-side filesystem paths discovered via probe (compose dir, log dir, etc.)
  - SSH connection shapes (user, port, key, ProxyJump if any) — cite ~/bin/rc-deployments.json entry
  - File-ownership and writability defaults on host-side and container-side
  - Named-volume layout (which paths persist across recreate, which don't)
  - Any entrypoint-specific behaviors (which env vars trigger refreshes, which steps are idempotent)
  - Any naming conventions you established (backup filenames, log file names)

  On subsequent dispatches against the same substrate, READ your scratchpad before probing — if a fact is already captured, use it; only re-probe if the fact looks stale or if the dispatch text explicitly directs a re-probe.

  Wiki-grade promotion: when a substrate-fact stabilizes (same entry across 2+ dispatches with no contradiction), Cal-Protocol-A submit as a substrate reference. Scratchpad is your working memory; the wiki is the team's. The pattern itself — `[LEARNED — substrate, <team-name>]` scratchpad-capture-then-promote — is also Cal-worthy once you've done it 2+ times across distinct substrates.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCOPE BOUNDARY (hard NO)

You will NOT:
- Recreate, restart, stop, start, or pull the apex-research container.
- Execute any docker exec into apex-research beyond what is implicit in P1.5b's `docker compose config` (which is host-side and inspects compose file + .env only).
- Modify any container-runtime state.
- Touch any FR-shipped substrate other than apex-research.
- Proceed past any [SURFACE-BACK] gate without my response.

If at any step a probe reveals the dispatch is wrong, or the substrate state contradicts the assumption baked into the dispatch, surface back immediately — do not patch the plan from your own diagnostic judgment. The cost of a round-trip is small; the cost of silent-broadening is the failure mode this dispatch's discipline exists to prevent.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SANCTION SUMMARY (validate against your prompt's Sanction Discipline section before executing)

- P1.1 — Tier R, default-permitted
- P1.2 — Tier R, default-permitted
- P1.3 — Tier M, tasker single-line ack quoted in the step body above
- P1.4 — Tier M, tasker single-line ack quoted in the step body above
- P1.5 — Tier R, default-permitted
- P1.5b — Tier R, default-permitted
- P1.6 — Tier R + log-write to your own MAY-WRITE path
- P1.6.5 — scratchpad write to your own MAY-WRITE path (teams/framework-research/memory/hopper.md)

No Tier D in this dispatch. If you find yourself classifying any step as Tier D after deployed-artifacts read, that's a hard-gate stop — surface to me.

— Brunel

[2026-05-20 17:13] Hopper — surface-back received and well-disciplined. My filter regex was wrong; the .env.example:13 stub comment (`michelek`) is template-only, not preserved in the operational .env. Below is the corrected P1.1 with rationale. CC'ing Aen on this thread for visibility per pair-as-unit reporting.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSIS OF THE REGEX DEFECT

I anchored on `.env.example:13` which shows `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY="ssh-ed25519 AAAA... michelek"` as a template. The live PO key comment is `mihkel.putrinsh@evr.ee apex-research` — neither token from the template survives. Operationally, PO uses his own pubkey with its own comment, not the template stub. The template was illustrative, not specification. Lesson for my own scratchpad later: when filtering on substrate-content, sample the actual substrate content if available; templates are stub-grade, not selector-grade.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
P1.1 AMENDMENT — positive-select `aleksandr` + belt-and-suspenders safety check

Option A (positive-select aleksandr) is the cleanest match for this dispatch's specific live state. The captured probe output you shared shows:

- LINE 1: `... mihkel.putrinsh@evr.ee apex-research` — PO's key, confirmed by `mihkel.putrinsh` (PO's name) and substrate-host identifier.
- LINE 2: `... ghost-bridge@aleksandr-2026-05-15` — Aleksandr's key. The `aleksandr-2026-05-15` token is unmistakable; `ghost-bridge@` prefix matches the cross-team-PoC ghost-member shape Aleksandr's team operates by.

Belt-and-suspenders against future failure modes: positive-select on `aleksandr` AND assert the captured line does NOT contain `mihkel`. Both checks must pass.

REPLACE the P1.1 body with this:

```powershell
$allKeys = ssh -i $env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_apex -p 2222 ai-teams@100.96.54.170 'cat /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys'
$alekCandidates = @($allKeys | Where-Object { $_ -match 'aleksandr' -and $_ -notmatch 'mihkel' -and $_.Trim() -ne '' })
if ($alekCandidates.Count -ne 1) {
    Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] Expected exactly 1 aleksandr-matching key, found $($alekCandidates.Count). Aborting dispatch."
    # halt — do not proceed to P1.2
}
$alekKey = $alekCandidates[0].TrimEnd("`r","`n"," ","`t")
```

Same hard-gate semantics: exactly 1 line passes; `@()` force-array preserves; `TrimEnd` strips CR/LF/whitespace; surface-back on 0 or 2+. The filter is the only thing that changed.

Pass criterion: $alekKey is set to a non-empty single-line string ending with `ghost-bridge@aleksandr-2026-05-15` (or comparable — what matters is exactly one match, and the candidate carries the `aleksandr` token).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SANCTION + SCOPE

This is still Tier R (default-permitted), no scope change to the dispatch as a whole. The amendment is regex-correction, not re-classification. Continue executing per the rest of r3 dispatch unchanged — P1.2 through P1.6.5 stand as written.

Once you have $alekKey captured, hard-gate-clear, proceed to P1.2.

If the new filter ALSO surfaces an unexpected count (i.e., 0 because `aleksandr` token isn't there at all, or 2+ because there's somehow another `aleksandr` key), surface back again — I'll need to look at the substrate more directly with you (probably via Option B you proposed: read host-side .env to disambiguate).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
OPS-LOG NOTE

Your ops-log entry should reflect the amendment trail:
- P1.1 surface-back at 17:12 (regex defect, 2-line ambiguity)
- Brunel amendment at 17:13 (positive-select aleksandr + mihkel-exclusion)
- P1.1 second-attempt execution timestamp
- The original regex `michelek\s*$` quoted verbatim from r3 + the amended filter quoted verbatim
This makes the diagnostic-loop traceable on the audit surface — exactly the kind of detail your prompt's verbatim-log discipline exists to preserve.

Go. — Brunel (*FR:Brunel*)

**Adopting your Option B (docker inspect compose-project-working-dir label).** Reasoning: cheap Tier R substrate-truth probe beats naming-convention inference when both are available. The substrate itself answers the question via its own labels — no inference layer.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
P1.2 DISAMBIGUATION PROBE — sanctioned, run as part of P1.2 (still Tier R, within-dispatch agency)

```powershell
$composeDirFromLabel = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 'docker inspect apex-research --format "{{ index .Config.Labels \"com.docker.compose.project_working_dir\" }}" 2>/dev/null'
```

Expected output: a single absolute path matching one of your two HIT candidates.

ALEK_KEY="`$(echo '$alekKeyB64' | base64 -d)"

set +a

SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3="`$RC_KEY"

GITHUB_TOKEN="`$GITHUB_TOKEN"
GH_TOKEN="`$GH_TOKEN_VAL"
ATLASSIAN_EMAIL="`$ATLASSIAN_EMAIL"
ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN="`$ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN"
ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL="`$ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL"
TUNNEL_TOKEN="`$TUNNEL_TOKEN"

"@

To activate the new `.env`, the container must be recreated — that's the canonical lifecycle event where compose reads `.env` and entrypoint Step 7 re-runs to write `authorized_keys`.

This is Tier D: `--force-recreate` destroys the running container and creates a new one. Any live SSH sessions to apex drop. Apex team is offline precisely so this drop has no operational impact on their work.

P4.0 substrate-truth (Hopper, 19:31): operational compose-yml's apex-research env block declares 14 vars: HOME, REPO_URL, SOURCE_REPO_URL, GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS, TEAM_NAME, ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL. ### (a) Exact command

```

### (b) Stated reason

Without recreate, the new `.env` is dormant and the substrate keeps serving on the stale baked-in env. Recreate is the canonical lifecycle event the substrate is designed for, but `--force-recreate` is Tier D because (i) it destroys the running container and all in-process state, and (ii) live SSH sessions drop. Apex team is offline by design to make this drop harmless; that's the precondition that elevates this from "impossible without coordination" to "safe-in-the-window."

The alternative — leave `.env` written but never recreate — would mean Aleksandr's key persistence remains unaddressed (the original ask) and the substrate stays in fragile state (any unplanned restart still causes the credential cascade). ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROOT CAUSE ACK — Sub-shape A n=4 self-instance

My 19:38 awk script had `print "      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"`. The PowerShell heredoc backtick-escaping converted `` `${...} `` to `${...}` correctly for bash, but awk's string parser saw `}` and either treated it as block-terminator OR parsed the unbalanced quoting differently. Either way, the literal-string I wanted awk to print contained a substring that awk's grammar didn't tolerate.

**You caught Sub-shape A n=4 in real time.** Same class as P1.1 michelek-regex / P1.2a label-key-typo / P3.6 character-class — discriminator/substrate-literal anchored on assumed-substrate-truth (assumed PowerShell→bash→awk would pass `${GH_TOKEN:-}` through as inert text) rather than verified-substrate-truth (awk grammar parses `}` specially). Now at the awk-layer of a multi-layer escape chain (PowerShell → base64 → bash heredoc → awk). Pattern is hardening.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
THE FIX — printf with separated string literals

Replace the line:
```awk
/- GITHUB_TOKEN=/ && in_apex { print; print "      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"; next }
```

with:
```awk
/- GITHUB_TOKEN=/ && in_apex { print; printf "      - GH_TOKEN=%s%s\n", "$", "{GH_TOKEN:-}"; next }
```

awk sees `"$"` and `"{GH_TOKEN:-}"` as two distinct, parser-safe string literals. printf joins them in the output. The `{GH_TOKEN:-}` chunk has no leading `$` so awk doesn't try to interpret it as anything special; it's just a bare-curly-brace literal which awk's string parser handles fine.

Output line in the rewritten compose-yml will be exactly:
```
      - GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}
```

Which is what compose-yml needs to interpolate `$GH_TOKEN` from `.env` at compose-up time.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HOUSEKEEPING — YES, `rm -f docker-compose.yml.new` at start

Cleaner state. The 0-byte leftover from yesterday's awk-fail is harmless but having it documented-and-removed at the top of the script makes the audit-trail clear ("amendment-2 starts with a clean substrate"). Sanctioned as part of the Tier M op; same Brunel ack covers it.

cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research'

# Find apex-research's environment block; insert GH_TOKEN line after GITHUB_TOKEN within that block only.
# Use printf with separated string literals to avoid awk parsing \${...} as a grammar-special.
awk '
  /^  apex-research:/ { in_apex=1 }
  /^  cloudflared:/ { in_apex=0 }
  /- GITHUB_TOKEN=/ && in_apex { print; printf "      - GH_TOKEN=%s%s\n", "$", "{GH_TOKEN:-}"; next }
  { print }
' docker-compose.yml > docker-compose.yml.new

# Sanity: .new is non-empty (i.e., awk produced output)
